policy.description: Blocking line-coverage ratchets. Update measured counts in the same reviewed change whenever scope or coverage advances; never lower a floor without an explicit, justified scope change.

tolerance.rationale: Two clean local Node 22/V8 coverage runs on 2026-07-17 passed 1244 tests, and the final clean run passed 1249 tests; all three measured the identical complete 2253-line core scope at 1932 covered lines (85.752%). Issue #122's localized/late home-row regressions exercise existing navigation and action-sheet paths, advancing 17 covered lines over the reviewed 1915/2253 baseline without changing scope. The existing one-line tolerance is unchanged and permits only the previously reproduced negligible instrumentation variance; coverage-baseline.test.js retains exact below-floor, above-baseline, scope-change, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.

Anime filler warnings implementation for project #371-#378 after independent adversarial hardening. Three consecutive clean local .NET 10.0.9/Coverlet integration runs on 2026-07-19 each passed all 1775 tests over the identical complete 28401-line assembly scope; two measured the reviewed high-water of 20013 covered lines (70.466%) and one measured 20012. Direct XML comparison isolated the one-line low-side difference to the pre-existing concurrent ImageBlurService cleanup path at line 740, outside this feature. The legitimate 623-line scope increase over 27778 adds the authenticated caller-scoped batch classification controller, bounded manual mapping parser, strict MAL/AniList/Jikan matching provider, discriminated transient indirect-resolution failures, a globally bounded waiter-aware shared-flight coordinator with final-waiter cancellation and operation deadlines, conservative per-origin rate gates with provider backoff headers and a 60-second headerless-429 fallback, response-body-inclusive request deadlines, bounded success/negative/error/last-good caches, fixed-origin HTTP integration, configuration, DI, and response contracts. Focused behavioral tests cover request bounds and order, caller-scoped episode and series resolution, absolute numbering, diagnostics redaction, direct/AniList/title precedence, wrong-year and malformed-ID rejection, provider pagination and indeterminate fields, retryable indirect-provider failures, controllable advised and unadvised rate backoff, stalled response bodies, stale last-good behavior, capacity rejection, cancellation, and concurrent waiter ownership; covered lines advanced by 566 while the aggregate percentage rose from 70.009% to 70.466%. The unchanged two-line low-side tolerance covers only the previously reproduced Coverlet instrumentation variance; coverage-baseline.test.js retains the exact scope-drift, regression, stale-baseline, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.
